newsSinceJun6: No material fundamental news since June 6. Routine insider sale by CPO Brandi Morandi (3,726 shares, June 8) and a boilerplate Halper Sadeh fiduciary-duty investigation press release (June 5) are noise. Citi added EQIX to its Focus List on June 3 (pre-window, already in price). Backdrop unchanged: record Q1 2026, raised FY26 guidance on AI demand, ~60% of largest deals AI-tied, 8 of top 10 AI model providers expanding in-facility, liquid-cooling deployments +50% q/q. Stock flat today (-0.03%) after +39% YTD.

sources.label: GuruFocus - Citi Focus List / Buy on EQIX (Jun 3)
Simply Wall St - EQIX valuation after raised AI-demand guidance
AI Consulting Network - Equinix Q1 2026 AI inference demand

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved EQIX up on the AI colocation evidence; nothing genuinely new has emerged since June 6 and the stock is flat today, so the setup is unchanged. The Citi Focus List add and the +39% YTD rerating slightly erode the under-owned residual alpha this page rewards, hence small negative drift rather than another move.
verdicts.bull: Equinix is a true physical bottleneck: 46 active builds across 32 markets, $4.1B 2026 capex, 8 of the top 10 AI model providers expanding in-facility, and liquid-cooling retrofits growing 50% q/q while AI-grade colocation supply stays scarce.
verdicts.neutral: Record Q1 and raised FY26 guidance are real but already disclosed and rewarded; at $104.6B market cap and +39% YTD with sell-side Focus List coverage, this is a well-followed mega-cap, not a hidden bottleneck play.
verdicts.bear: The rerating has front-run the fundamentals: any slowdown in AI inference colocation bookings, power-procurement delays, or REIT rate sensitivity hits a stock priced for continued beat-and-raise, and insiders are trimming.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings and bookings update (AI deal mix, atNorth integration, capacity/power adds) plus any new hyperscaler or neocloud capacity commitments.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding after the +39% YTD run; rate-driven REIT multiple compression or AI colocation bookings deceleration would unwind the rerating with little residual alpha cushion.
